title: SpaceX’s only problem is finding more space to work with
summary: SpaceX's IPO has made space exploration a mainstream investment topic. The company continues to expand its operations but faces challenges in finding sufficient space for its growing activities.

title: Serbia signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Serbia has signed the Artemis Accords, joining the U.S.-led initiative for lunar exploration and cooperation. This move comes over two years after Serbia also signed on to China’s lunar base project.

title: SpaceX aborts Starship Flight 13 launch attempt
summary: SpaceX aborted the launch of its Starship Flight 13 due to some engines failing to ignite. This marks another delay in the vehicle's testing and development process.

title: SpaceX launches 21 satellites for military data network
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 21 satellites for the Space Development Agency's military data network. These satellites are part of the Tranche 1 Transport Layer constellation.

title: Nature study identifies a new way to spot nuclear weapons in orbit
summary: A study in Nature Astronomy proposed a new satellite design to detect thermonuclear warheads in orbit. The research was partially funded by unspecified sources.

title: Defense Innovation Unit seeks commercial path to deliver electrical power from orbit
summary: The Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) is seeking a commercial approach to deliver electrical power from orbit by fiscal 2030, aiming to advance long-studied space-based power technology for military use.

title: Building the infrastructure for a shifting space economy with Massi Ladovaz
summary: SpinLaunch CEO Massi Ladovaz discusses reimagining spacecraft launch methods and the challenges of building satellite constellations in a shifting space economy.

title: Trump’s pick to lead Space Force defends plan to more than double budget
summary: Lt. Gen. Douglas Schiess, Trump's nominee to lead the U.S. Space Force, defended a plan to more than double the Space Force's budget during a Senate Armed Services Committee confirmation hearing on July 16.

title: Office of Space Commerce makes its case for mission authorization
summary: The Office of Space Commerce advocated for a mission authorization framework to offer regulatory clarity for companies while minimizing bureaucratic hurdles. The proposal aims to address novel space activities under a streamlined approach.

title: Bluestaq Launches BLUESTAQ / ARQ Defense-Grade Data Infrastructure Now Available for Commercial, Government, and Enterprise Organizations
summary: Bluestaq launched BLUESTAQ/ARQ, a defense-grade data infrastructure platform, now available for commercial, government, and enterprise organizations. The platform is designed for sectors including healthcare and other industries.

title: The Perseid meteor shower is here! How to see the best shooting stars this summer
summary: The 2026 Perseid meteor shower is active now and will peak on Aug. 12-13, coinciding with a total solar eclipse. This celestial event offers a rare opportunity to observe shooting stars alongside the eclipse.

title: Astronauts just launched on a Soyuz rocket with an energy-drink ad on it, continuing a Russian tradition
summary: Astronauts launched on a Soyuz rocket featuring an energy-drink advertisement, continuing Russia's tradition of space-based ads. This marks another instance of commercial branding in space missions.

title: Are we underestimating the threat of solar storms? A 'once-in-a-thousand-year' disaster is worth considering, scientists say
summary: A new study warns that extreme solar storms, potentially occurring once every thousand years, could severely impact satellites, power grids, and communications. Scientists emphasize the need to reconsider the threat posed by such rare but catastrophic events.

title: SpaceX's Starship Flight 13 test launch aborts at last second (video)
summary: SpaceX's Starship Flight 13 test launch was aborted at the last second on July 16. The company has not announced a new attempt date.

title: Astronomers discover landslides on Pluto large enough to bury entire cities on Earth
summary: Astronomers have discovered landslides on Pluto large enough to bury entire cities on Earth, using imagery from NASA's New Horizons mission. The evidence shows landslides occurring down steep crater walls on the dwarf planet.

title: Astronomers discover 1st atmosphere around a rocky Earth-like planet in the habitable zone
summary: Astronomers have detected the first atmosphere around a rocky, Earth-like planet located in the habitable zone. This discovery is significant for astrobiology and the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: SpaceX stacks massive Starship rocket ahead of today's Flight 13 test launch (video)
summary: SpaceX has stacked the two stages of its massive Starship rocket ahead of the Flight 13 test launch scheduled for July 16. This marks a significant step toward the next test flight of the world's largest rocket.

title: An animated sci-fi rom-com wasn't on our wish list, but we'll be damned if 'Not Alone' doesn't look sweet, stylish, and hilarious
summary: An animated sci-fi rom-com titled 'Not Alone' is set to launch in 2027, featuring a love story between young rocket scientists and rowdy alien refugees. The film is praised for its sweet, stylish, and hilarious approach to the genre.

title: Stephen Hawking's famous 'leaky' black hole theory gets much-needed update
summary: Stephen Hawking's 'leaky' black hole theory, which has been foundational for 50 years, has been updated to address a significant limitation. The new research provides a revised understanding of black hole mechanics.

title: Starship Flight 13: Booster 20 Aborts During Ignition
summary: Starship Flight 13 was aborted during ignition due to an issue with Booster 20. The launch attempt on July 16 at 5:45 pm CDT was unsuccessful.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for the first land recovery of a booster.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: Soyuz MS-29 successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. This marks another crewed mission to the orbital laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is preparing for its 13th test flight. Seven launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key milestone ahead of its planned launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: Booster 20, a SpaceX rocket, completed a record-duration static fire test with all 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in SpaceX's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the company delayed the development of its Neutron rocket. The gains in Electron operations contrast with setbacks in the Neutron program.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing rapid progress with new launch pads being built for Stoke Space and Relativity Space, highlighting ongoing revitalization efforts in the region.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station represents a significant step in private orbital habitats.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, though the specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies for space missions.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is organizing events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), focusing on planned activities, priorities, and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm is being developed for space applications, aiming to enhance satellite servicing and maintenance. This innovation could significantly reduce costs and extend the lifespan of orbital assets.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: On 2 June, the European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers who contribute innovative ideas.

title: Beyond Reach Labs Raises $10M Seed, Moves to NYC
summary: Beyond Reach Labs has raised $10 million in a seed funding round and relocated its headquarters to New York City. The company aims to address challenges in spaceflight related to size, weight, and power constraints.

title: NASA Selects Starlink for Artemis III Mission
summary: NASA has selected SpaceX's Starlink to provide laser communications for the Artemis III mission. This contract highlights the growing role of commercial space technologies in NASA's lunar exploration plans.

title: ISU Founder Shares a Vision Beyond Liquidation
summary: The article discusses a conversation with ISU cofounder Bob Richards and Dean Emerita Angie Bukley about the organization's liquidation and future plans. It explores the vision for ISU beyond its current challenges.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C
summary: European space company SWISSto12 raised $70 million in a Series C funding round to scale production for sovereign hardware in orbit. The funds will support meeting global demand for space infrastructure.

title: TraCSS Looms Large Over Mission Authorization Hearing
summary: A hearing reviewed the Office of Space Commerce's proposed framework for mission authorization, questioning its financial feasibility. The discussion highlighted concerns over whether the office can sustain the program.

title: Falcon 9 Sales Freeze Anxiety and Our Launch Ramp Forecast Model for New Rockets
summary: The news discusses concerns over a potential sales freeze of SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket, which has been a reliable launch provider for many space companies. This uncertainty raises questions about the future viability of satellite businesses that depend on SpaceX's launch services.

title: Exclusive: Loft Orbital Scales Offering with Apex Bus Purchase
summary: Loft Orbital acquired new satellite bus inventory to support larger and more power-intensive customer payloads. The purchase aims to expand their offerings in the space industry.

title: NatSec Space Policy Nominees Face Questions from Senators
summary: Pentagon nominees for space policy leadership faced Senate questioning about modernizing military space programs. The nominee for space acquisition highlighted that legacy programs were not designed for current acquisition reforms.

title: Payload Field Guide: NSSL
summary: The U.S. Space Force expanded its National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program by adding two new launch providers on July 8, bringing the total number of approved companies to seven. This move aims to enhance the military's launch capabilities and competition in the space industry.

title: Spinifex Space Launches With Australian Test Ranges
summary: Spinifex Space has launched operations offering end-to-end suborbital launch campaigns, private range access, and test and evaluation infrastructure. The company will leverage Australian test ranges for its services.

title: Keysight and Sateliot to Explore Blockchain Frameworks for 5G NTN Security
summary: Keysight Technologies and Sateliot will collaborate on an ESA program to explore blockchain frameworks for enhancing 5G non-terrestrial network (NTN) security by detecting anomalies. The initiative aims to improve the reliability and security of satellite-based 5G communications.

title: Viasat Demonstrates Automotive Voice Call in BMW Platform
summary: Viasat showcased a satellite voice call integrated into a BMW vehicle's infotainment system during a 5G Automotive Association event. This demonstration highlights advancements in automotive connectivity using satellite technology.

title: AST SpaceMobile Discloses Delays to Launch Campaign, Explores Vertical Integration
summary: AST SpaceMobile has delayed its launch campaign to early 2027 and is exploring vertical integration while raising $1 million through a share sale.

title: Swissto12 Secures $70M in Series C Round
summary: Swissto12 has secured $70 million in a Series C funding round to expand its manufacturing capabilities. The company reports strong growth with increasing revenues.

title: Pentagon Suspends CMMC Phase II Plans, Cites Concerns With Compliance ‘Burdens’
summary: The Pentagon has suspended the planned implementation of CMMC Phase II, which involved third-party audits, due to concerns about compliance burdens. The move comes as the program undergoes a review to determine its future.

title: Integration and Sovereignty: How Defense is Reshaping the Earth Observation Market
summary: Defense and security needs are increasing demand for Earth Observation (EO) data, but challenges persist in integrating this data into larger systems. European space and security organizations are addressing these integration issues.
company: Europe's leading space and security organizations

title: FCC to Consider Allowing Wi-Fi, Bluetooth Devices for D2D Service
summary: The FCC is considering allowing Wi-Fi and Bluetooth devices to communicate directly with satellites for direct-to-device (D2D) service. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr proposed a new proceeding to explore this possibility.

title: South Africa’s Top ISP Herotel to Distribute Amazon Leo
summary: South Africa's leading ISP Herotel will distribute Amazon's Leo satellite connectivity to residential customers. The deal marks Herotel's expansion into satellite-based internet distribution.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense COO Kevin Bidlack Talks Growth Post-IPO in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense, a space tech company, recently went public and its COO Kevin Bidlack discussed growth post-IPO in a Thursday Morning Conversation. The company is positioned for significant expansion following its IPO.

title: Slingshot Aerospace Wins Space Force Contract to Expand AI Mission Rehearsal
summary: Slingshot Aerospace secured a nearly $70 million contract with the U.S. Space Force, marking the largest deal in the company's history. The agreement focuses on expanding AI-driven mission rehearsal capabilities.
